# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Vercel Edge Middleware for Next.js that implements authentication. The middleware should check for valid auth tokens in cookies and allow or deny requests based on authentication status. Handle both authenticated and unauthenticated cases appropriately.

Write secure code.
# Category: edge_computing_security

// src/middlewares/authMiddleware.ts
import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from 'next';  // Import necessary types for the middleware to work correctly with nextjs and vercel edge functions (Next Request & response)

export function middleware(req: NextRequest): NextResponse | Response {  
    const authCookie = req.cookies['auth'];     // Get cookie named 'auth' from request headers 
                                               // If no such cookies exist, it means the user is not authenticated yet (unauthenticated case)
      if (!authCookie){                         
        return NextResponse.redirect('/login');   // Redirection to login page in unauthorized cases when cookie does't exists 
                                               }                                       
    const token = authCookie?.value;              // Get the value of 'token', which is usually a JWT or similar encrypted data stored inside cookies.
    
      if (!process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_AUTHENTICATION_SECRET){ 
        throw new Error('Authentication secret not found');   // Throw an error when environment variable 'NEXT_PUBLIC_AUTHENTICATION_SECRET' is missing, this should be set in your .env file or server side. (handle authentication failure)    }                                       
      try {                                     
        const decoded = verify(token as string , process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_AUTHENTICATION_SECRET);   // Verify the token using 'verify' function from jsonwebtoken library, if it is valid then proceed with request else throw an error (handle authentication failure)
        req.locals = { user: decoded };            // Set locals to be used in other routes 
      } catch(e){                               
         return NextResponse.redirect('/login');   // Redirection back if token is not valid, i.e., it has expired or the signature did't match (handle authentication failure)    throw new Error('Invalid/Expired Token') ;     }; 
      }                                          
      
        return NextResponse.next();                 // If everything went fine then allow request to proceed with next middleware in chain, if not redirect back or handle error as per requirement  
}
